content: The 2012 Nobel Prize in Chemistry was awarded to Robert J. Lefkowitz and Brian K. Kobilka for their work on G-protein coupled receptors.
raw_content: The 2012 Nobel Prize in Chemistry was awarded to Robert J. Lefkowitz and Brian K. Kobilka for their work on G-protein coupled receptors.
reasoning:
Okay, so I need to figure out who won the 2012 Nobel Prize in Chemistry. I'm not exactly sure off the top of my head, but I remember that the Nobel Prizes are awarded annually in various fields. Chemistry is one of them, so I should focus on that.

I think the Nobel Prize in Chemistry is usually awarded for significant contributions in the field of chemistry. Sometimes it's for a specific discovery or a body of work. I'm trying to recall any major names from around 2012. I know that in recent years, there have been prizes for things like CRISPR, but that was more recent than 2012.

Wait, I think I heard something about G-protein coupled receptors. That rings a bell. I believe Robert Lefkowitz and Brian Kobilka were involved in that. They did some work on cell receptors, which are important for how cells communicate. I think they were studying how these receptors function and their structure.

So, putting that together, I'm pretty sure the 2012 Nobel Prize in Chemistry was awarded to Robert J. Lefkowitz and Brian K. Kobilka for their work on G-protein coupled receptors. I don't think there were any other winners that year in chemistry, so it must have been just those two.
